# Epic PRD Template

This is the canonical per-epic PRD template used by pm-prd-generator. Every epic produces one file following this structure, written to `{project}/prd/{epic-name}-prd.md`.

---

## Template Begin

```markdown
# {Epic Name} -- Product Requirements Document

**Version**: {1.0}
**Status**: Draft | In Review | Approved | Superseded
**Author**: {auto-generated by pm-prd-generator}
**Date**: {current date}
**Epic ID**: {EPIC-PREFIX from understanding doc or assigned}
**Input Mode**: {A -- SaaS Pipeline | B -- Consulting Pipeline | C -- Standalone}
**Parent Initiative**: {initiative name from understanding doc, or user-provided}

---

## 1. Problem Statement & Business Context

### 1.1 Business Problem

{2-3 paragraphs describing the business problem this epic addresses. Write from the perspective of the personas affected. Avoid technical jargon.}

### 1.2 Initiative Classification

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| **Type** | {New Product / Feature Enhancement / Operational Improvement / Compliance & Regulatory / Technical Debt / Platform Migration} |
| **Business Driver** | {Revenue Growth / Cost Reduction / Risk Mitigation / Operational Efficiency / Customer Retention / Regulatory Compliance} |
| **Strategic Alignment** | {Which company OKR, strategic pillar, or business goal does this serve?} |

### 1.3 Impact of Inaction

{What happens if this epic is not built? Quantify where possible: revenue at risk, compliance penalties, efficiency loss, customer churn rate.}

### 1.4 Upstream References

| Document | Location | Relevant Sections |
|----------|----------|------------------|
| Understanding Document | `{project}/discovery/understanding-doc.md` | {section references} |
| Process Flows | `{project}/discovery/process-flows/` | {specific flow files} |
| TOM Package (if Mode B) | `{project}/tom/` | {specific TOM artifacts} |

---

## 2. Stakeholders & Personas

### 2.1 Stakeholder Map

| Stakeholder | Role | Interest | Influence | Communication |
|-------------|------|----------|-----------|---------------|
| {Name/Title} | {Executive Sponsor / Business Owner / Technical Lead / End User} | {What they care about} | {High / Medium / Low} | {Frequency and channel} |
| | | | | |

### 2.2 Personas

<!-- MODE A (SaaS): Include behavior, feelings, journey stage -->

#### Persona: {Persona Name}

| Attribute | Detail |
|-----------|--------|
| **Role** | {job title and organizational context} |
| **Behavior Patterns** | {how they currently work, tools they use, frequency of interaction} |
| **Feelings & Frustrations** | {pain points, emotional state, what frustrates them about current process} |
| **Journey Stage** | {Awareness / Evaluation / Onboarding / Active Use / Power Use / At Risk} |
| **Goals** | {what success looks like for this persona} |
| **Engagement Metrics** | {DAU/WAU/MAU, session length, feature adoption rate -- SaaS specific} |

<!-- MODE B (Consulting): Include TOM role mapping -->

#### Persona: {Persona Name}

| Attribute | Detail |
|-----------|--------|
| **Role** | {job title and organizational context} |
| **TOM Role Mapping** | {GPO / Process Owner / Business Analyst / System User / Approver} |
| **L2 Process Ownership** | {which L2 processes this persona owns or participates in} |
| **Current Pain Points** | {operational inefficiencies, manual workarounds, compliance gaps} |
| **Change Impact** | {High / Medium / Low -- how much this persona's work changes} |
| **Training Needs** | {what new skills or knowledge this persona needs} |

<!-- Repeat persona block for each persona (minimum 2 per epic) -->

---

## 3. TOM Alignment

<!-- THIS SECTION IS INCLUDED ONLY FOR MODE B (CONSULTING PIPELINE) -->
<!-- For Mode A (SaaS) and Mode C (Standalone), omit this entire section -->

### 3.1 Process Mapping

| TOM Level | ID | Process Name | Current Maturity | Target Maturity | Gap |
|-----------|----|-------------|-----------------|----------------|-----|
| L1 | {id} | {value chain name} | {1-5} | {1-5} | {delta} |
| L2 | {id} | {process group name} | {1-5} | {1-5} | {delta} |
| L3 | {id} | {process name} | {1-5} | {1-5} | {delta} |
| L4 | {id} | {activity name} | {1-5} | {1-5} | {delta} |

### 3.2 Maturity Gap Closure

{For each mapped process with a gap > 0, describe:}

**{Process Name} ({Current} -> {Target})**
- Current state: {description of how this process works today}
- Target state: {description of how this process will work after this epic}
- Gap closure actions: {specific changes this epic delivers}
- Capability register reference: {ID from TOM workbook}

### 3.3 Organizational Impact

| Role | Current Responsibility | Future Responsibility | Change Type |
|------|----------------------|----------------------|-------------|
| {role name} | {what they do today} | {what they will do} | {New / Modified / Eliminated / Unchanged} |

---

## 4. Epic Definition

### 4.1 Epic Statement

```
As a {primary persona},
I want {high-level capability this epic delivers},
So that {business value achieved}.
```

### 4.2 Scope

#### In Scope

- {Explicit capability or feature included in this epic}
- {Another capability}
- {Be specific -- "user registration" not "user management"}

#### Out of Scope

- {Capability explicitly excluded, even if someone might assume it is included}
- {Adjacent feature that belongs in a different epic}
- {Future enhancement deferred to a later phase}

### 4.3 Dependencies

| Dependency | Type | Epic/System | Impact if Unavailable |
|-----------|------|-------------|----------------------|
| {description} | {Hard / Soft / Data} | {epic name or external system} | {what happens} |

### 4.4 Assumptions

1. {Assumption that, if false, would change the scope or approach}
2. {Another assumption -- each should be specific and falsifiable}
3. {Link to Open Questions section if assumption needs validation}

---

## 5. User Stories

<!-- Repeat this block for each user story. Order: MH first, then SH, then CH. -->
<!-- Within priority tier, order by dependency (foundational stories first). -->

### Story {EPIC-PREFIX}-{NNN}: {Story Title}

**Priority**: {MH / SH / CH} | **Complexity**: {S / M / L}

```
As a {persona name from Section 2},
I want {specific action the persona takes},
So that {specific value the persona receives}.
```

**Acceptance Criteria:**

```gherkin
AC-1: {Happy path}
Given {precondition}
When {action}
Then {expected result}

AC-2: {Validation / boundary}
Given {precondition}
When {action with edge case input}
Then {expected handling}

AC-3: {Error / negative scenario}
Given {precondition}
When {action that triggers error}
Then {error handling behavior}
```

**Notes:** {optional -- implementation hints, design references, open questions}

**Dependencies:** {optional -- other story IDs this depends on, e.g., "Requires EPIC-001 complete"}

---

<!-- EXAMPLE STORY (remove from generated PRDs) -->

### Story UO-001: New User Email Registration

**Priority**: MH | **Complexity**: S

```
As a prospective customer,
I want to create an account using my email address,
So that I can access the platform and start my onboarding journey.
```

**Acceptance Criteria:**

```gherkin
AC-1: Successful registration
Given I am on the registration page
When I enter a valid email, password meeting complexity rules, and click "Create Account"
Then my account is created, I receive a verification email, and I see the "Check your email" confirmation page

AC-2: Duplicate email
Given the email "jane@example.com" is already registered
When I attempt to register with "jane@example.com"
Then I see the message "An account with this email already exists" and a link to the login page

AC-3: Weak password
Given I am on the registration page
When I enter a password shorter than 8 characters or without a number
Then I see inline validation errors specifying the unmet requirements
  And the form is not submitted

AC-4: Verification email expiry
Given I registered but did not verify within 24 hours
When I click the verification link after 24 hours
Then I see "This link has expired" with an option to request a new verification email
```

**Notes:** Password complexity rules defined in security policy doc. Verification email template in design system.

---

## 6. Key Features & Business Value

| # | Feature | Description | Stories Covered | Business Value | Star Level (1-11) |
|---|---------|-------------|----------------|---------------|-------------------|
| 1 | {Feature name} | {1-sentence description} | {story IDs, e.g., UO-001, UO-002} | {Quantified business value} | {1-11} |
| 2 | | | | | |
| 3 | | | | | |

### Star Level Reference

The Star Level column uses the 11-star experience scale. This rating is consumed by `pm-prd-reviewer` to validate that the specified experience level is achievable and appropriate.

- **1-3**: Functional minimum. Gets the job done with no delight. Acceptable for internal tools or MVP.
- **4-6**: Expected experience. Meets industry standard. Users are satisfied but not impressed.
- **7-9**: Delightful experience. Exceeds expectations. Users notice and appreciate the quality.
- **10-11**: Transformative. Redefines user expectations for this category. Rare and intentional.

---

## 7. Success Metrics

| Metric | Baseline | Target | Measurement Method | Frequency |
|--------|----------|--------|-------------------|-----------|
| {metric name} | {current value or "N/A -- new"} | {target value with timeframe} | {tool, query, or survey} | {real-time / daily / weekly / monthly / quarterly} |
| | | | | |
| | | | | |

### Leading Indicators (predict success)

- {Metric that shows early signal -- e.g., "Registration completion rate in first week"}

### Lagging Indicators (confirm success)

- {Metric that confirms sustained value -- e.g., "30-day retention rate for registered users"}

---

## 8. Constraints & Assumptions

### 8.1 Constraints

| Category | Constraint | Impact |
|----------|-----------|--------|
| Technical | {e.g., "Must integrate with existing OAuth provider"} | {how it limits design choices} |
| Timeline | {e.g., "Must launch before Q3 regulatory deadline"} | {what gets cut if timeline slips} |
| Budget | {e.g., "No additional SaaS license spend in FY26"} | {limits technology choices} |
| Regulatory | {e.g., "GDPR data residency requirements for EU users"} | {architecture constraints} |
| Organizational | {e.g., "Only 2 frontend developers available"} | {limits parallel work} |

### 8.2 Assumptions

1. {Specific, falsifiable assumption}
2. {Another assumption}

### 8.3 Risk Register

| Risk ID | Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation | Owner |
|---------|------|-----------|--------|-----------|-------|
| R-001 | {description of what could go wrong} | {H / M / L} | {H / M / L} | {what we will do to prevent or respond} | {who owns this risk} |
| R-002 | | | | | |

---

## 9. Technical Considerations

> **Note**: This section is intentionally non-prescriptive. It provides context for the engineering team and downstream `spec` skill. It does not dictate architecture.

### 9.1 Integration Points

| System | Direction | Data Exchanged | Protocol | Notes |
|--------|-----------|---------------|----------|-------|
| {external system name} | {Inbound / Outbound / Bidirectional} | {data types} | {REST / GraphQL / Event / File} | {SLA, rate limits, auth method} |

### 9.2 Data Requirements

| Data Entity | Volume | Sensitivity | Retention | Notes |
|------------|--------|-------------|-----------|-------|
| {entity name} | {expected records/size} | {Public / Internal / Confidential / Highly Confidential} | {retention period} | {compliance requirements} |

### 9.3 Performance Expectations

| Scenario | Metric | Target |
|----------|--------|--------|
| {e.g., "Page load"} | {response time / throughput / concurrent users} | {target value} |

### 9.4 Security Requirements

- Authentication: {method required -- SSO, MFA, API key}
- Authorization: {model -- RBAC, ABAC, role definitions}
- Data protection: {encryption, masking, anonymization needs}
- Compliance: {SOC2, HIPAA, GDPR, PCI-DSS -- which apply}

### 9.5 Accessibility Requirements

- WCAG level: {AA / AAA}
- Supported devices: {desktop, tablet, mobile}
- Assistive technology: {screen reader, keyboard navigation}

---

## 10. Process Flow References

### 10.1 Primary Flow

<!-- Embed Mermaid diagram from pm-discovery or generate simplified version -->

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[{Start state}] --> B{Decision point}
    B -->|Yes| C[{Happy path action}]
    B -->|No| D[{Alternative path}]
    C --> E[{Next step}]
    D --> F[{Error handling}]
    E --> G[{End state}]
    F --> G
```

### 10.2 Alternative Flows

<!-- Additional flow diagrams for secondary paths, error flows, or edge cases -->

### 10.3 Flow Source

| Flow | Source | File |
|------|--------|------|
| {flow name} | pm-discovery | `{project}/discovery/process-flows/{filename}` |

---

## 11. Release & Rollout

### 11.1 Release Strategy

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| **Strategy** | {Big Bang / Phased Rollout / Feature Flag / Beta then GA / Canary} |
| **Initial Audience** | {who gets it first -- internal, beta users, specific segment} |
| **GA Target** | {date or sprint for general availability} |

### 11.2 Rollout Phases (if phased)

| Phase | Audience | Criteria to Advance | Duration |
|-------|----------|-------------------|----------|
| 1 | {e.g., "Internal team"} | {e.g., "Zero P1 bugs for 48 hours"} | {e.g., "1 week"} |
| 2 | {e.g., "Beta customers (100)"} | {e.g., "NPS > 30, error rate < 0.1%"} | {e.g., "2 weeks"} |
| 3 | {e.g., "All customers"} | {e.g., "Metrics stable for 1 week"} | {Permanent} |

### 11.3 Rollback Criteria

| Trigger | Action | Owner |
|---------|--------|-------|
| {e.g., "Error rate exceeds 1%"} | {e.g., "Disable feature flag, revert to previous version"} | {team or individual} |
| {e.g., "P1 bug affecting payments"} | {e.g., "Immediate rollback, incident response"} | {team or individual} |

### 11.4 Communication Plan

| Audience | Channel | Timing | Message |
|----------|---------|--------|---------|
| {e.g., "All users"} | {e.g., "In-app banner + email"} | {e.g., "1 week before launch"} | {summary of what is changing} |

---

## 12. Open Questions

| ID | Question | Owner | Due Date | Status | Resolution |
|----|----------|-------|----------|--------|------------|
| OQ-001 | {specific, answerable question} | {person responsible} | {YYYY-MM-DD} | {Open / In Progress / Resolved} | {answer, when resolved} |
| OQ-002 | | | | | |

---

## Document History

| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
|---------|------|--------|---------|
| 1.0 | {date} | pm-prd-generator | Initial PRD generation |

---

*Generated by pm-prd-generator v1.0 | Input Mode: {A/B/C} | Epic: {epic-name}*
```

## Template End

---

## Usage Notes

1. **Conditional sections**: Section 3 (TOM Alignment) is only included for Mode B. Remove the entire section (not just leave it blank) for Mode A and Mode C.
2. **Persona blocks**: Use the SaaS persona template for Mode A, the Consulting persona template for Mode B. For Mode C, use whichever is more appropriate based on context.
3. **Story count**: Minimum 3 stories per epic, maximum 15. If decomposition yields more than 15, split the epic.
4. **Star Level**: Must be assigned to every feature. The reviewer uses this for validation.
5. **Open Questions**: Do not leave this empty. If there are genuinely no open questions, state "No open questions at time of generation" as a single row.
6. **Mermaid diagrams**: Use the exact diagram syntax from pm-discovery where available. Generate simplified versions only when upstream diagrams do not exist.

---

*pm-prd-generator v1.0 | Epic PRD Template*
